title: Tech Disruptors: Nicole Hu on Google TurboQuant and Evolving AI
summary: As hyperscalers look to improve the efficiency of AI workloads, advances in quantization are redefining the tradeoffs between memory and compute, with far-reaching implications for cost, latency, and datacenter architecture. Bloomberg Intelligence Head of Technology Research Mandeep Singh is joined by Nicole Hu, a Silicon Valley technology veteran and GLG expert, to explore the implications of Google’s TurboQuant paper and the evolving economics of AI infrastructure. title: ESG Currents: Investing in the $20 Trillion Climate Economy
summary: Global costs related to climate events, including damage, insurance premiums, maintenance and repair, have exceeded $20 trillion since the year 2000, according to Bloomberg Intelligence’s Climate Damages Tracker. This massive wealth transfer provides the context for BI’s Climate Economy 2026 Outlook report. Emily Chang meets 23andMe Research Institute CEO Anne Wojcicki for a discussion of the rise, fall and rebirth of the personal genetics company.

title: Switzerland Still in Talks With US as Trump Proposes New Tariffs
summary: Switzerland said it remains in talks with the US after Washington proposed fresh tariffs on imports from major trading partners, adding that negotiations to finalize a broader trade deal are continuing.

title: Homeownership Isn’t Working Out for British Millenials
summary:
The dream of climbing the property ladder isn't working out for British millennials.

Olivia Rudgard explains why young UK owners are finding themselves stuck. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Slip as Middle East Flare-Ups Lift Oil: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks struggled to build on record gains as growing strains on the ceasefire between the US and Iran sent oil prices higher for a third straight day and lifted bond yields.

title: Raizen Submits $13 Billion Debt Overhaul Plan to Creditors
summary: Brazil’s Raizen SA presented creditors with a final restructuring proposal, a key step in the struggling sugar and ethanol producer’s months-long push to clinch an out-of-court debt overhaul.

title: US Proposes New Tariffs After Investigation; US, Iran Exchange Clashes | Bloomberg Brief 6/3/2026
summary: US equity futures hover above another record close and oil climbs amid clashes between the US and Iran. The US proposes new tariffs of at least 10% on imports from 60 trading partners after an investigation into the handling of goods. David Layton, CEO of Partners Group, discusses the redemption pressure seen among individual investors as the private equity firm caps withdrawals at on one of its evergreen funds. Mark McCormick of BMO Capital Markets discusses the strength of the US dollar. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bahrain Taps Dollar Bond Market Hours After Missile Attack
summary: Bahrain is marketing a dollar bond hours after fending off Iranian missile attacks, in a key test of investor appetite for Middle East debt.

title: EU Allows Room for Energy Crisis Aid, But With a Green Emphasis
summary: The European Union will give extra budget leeway to member states to cope with high energy prices stoked by the Iran war, so long as it helps curb consumption of fossil fuels.

title: Yen Holds Near 160 Against Dollar After BOJ’s Ueda Hints at Hike
summary: The yen remained near the 160 level versus the dollar following remarks by Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda that make an interest rate hike this month sound likely but not certain.

title: Druckenmiller Leads Wall Street’s Return to Argentine Stocks
summary: Foreign investors led by the likes of Stanley Druckenmiller and major Wall Street banks are returning to Argentine stocks this year after some had exited ahead of 2025’s volatile midterm election cycle.

title: JPMorgan’s Lipikhina Sees Earnings Supercycle Driving US Stocks
summary: An earnings “supercycle” in the US is poised to drive stocks to more record highs, according to Nataliia Lipikhina at JPMorgan Chase & Co., fueled by heavy spending from hyperscalers and advances in agentic AI.

title: Korea, Taiwan Stock Rallies Fail to Wow Foreign Investors
summary: Cash is flowing into Japanese equities.

title: Hedge Fund Targets Ferrari Owners in Private Credit Exotics Boom
summary: Private credit’s getting into the luxury car business.

title: Lee’s Party on Track for Landslide in South Korea Local Election
summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung’s party is on track for a resounding victory in key local elections that is likely to pile pressure on opposition conservatives as they struggle to reinvent their party after the debacle of their former leader’s martial law decree.

title: Gold Slips as Uncertainty Clouds Negotiations to End US-Iran War
summary: Gold declined as renewed clashes in the Middle East cast doubt over negotiations between the US and Iran to end the war that’s upended energy flows and heightened inflation concerns.

title: UK’s Ovo Energy to Make Payment for Failing Vulnerable Users
summary: UK energy supplier Ovo Energy Ltd. is set to pay £10.4 million ($14 million) after an investigation by the nation’s watchdog found its actions had put customers at risk.

title: Deutsche Bank Risks Needing More Than Expected for Soured Loans
summary: Deutsche Bank AG may set aside more money than analysts had expected for souring loans in the three months through June as it cleans up its balance sheet.

title: Pakistan Fuel Sales Slump as War Boosts Prices, Hits Demand
summary: Fuel sales in Pakistan have slumped as costlier prices triggered by the Iran war force consumers to cut back, according to AKD Securities Ltd.

title: Partners Caps Redemptions on Evergreen Fund Amid Rising Requests
summary: Partners Group CEO David Layton discusses the firm’s move to cap withdrawals at one of its evergreen private equity funds amid heightened redemption pressure. He speaks with Matt Miller on “Bloomberg Brief.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Begins Rebuilding His Tariff Wall Citing Forced Labor
summary: The US is proposing new tariffs of at least 10% on imports from 60 trading partners in President Donald Trump’s biggest move to rebuild his protectionist wall since his earlier levies were struck down by the Supreme Court.

title: India Stays ‘Engaged’ With US as Trump Proposes New Tariffs
summary: India said it remains in talks with the US after Washington proposed fresh tariffs on imports from major trading partners, adding that negotiations on a broader trade framework agreement announced earlier this year are continuing.

title: Nestle Buys Out Yfood Founders in First Acquisition for New CEO
summary: Nestle SA is taking full control of ready-to-drink meal maker yfood Labs GmbH, bolstering the company’s push to focus on faster-growing brands, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Indonesian Stocks Slump to Five-Year Low, Rupiah Drops to Record
summary: Indonesian stocks plunged to their lowest level in five years and the rupiah hit a record low in another stark reminder of the multi-faceted challenges confronting Southeast Asia’s biggest economy.

title: Treasuries Eye Biggest Drop In Two Weeks on Fed Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Treasuries slid, heading for their biggest drop in more than two weeks, after an escalation in US-Iran tensions lifted oil prices and added to expectations that the Federal Reserve may raise interest rates this year.

title: Broadcom’s $280 Billion, Four-Day Bonanza to Get Earnings Check
summary: Broadcom Inc. shares have been on a tear, adding more than $280 billion in market value in just the last four sessions. But with earnings arriving after the bell, investors will have a chance to assess whether the rally has gone too far.

title: Copper Drops From Three-Week High as US and Iran Exchange Fire
summary: Copper fell from a three-week high amid pessimism over the outlook for a deal to end the Iran war.

title: BNP Manager Who Slammed ‘Meme-Like’ SMR Rally Is Now a Buyer
summary: A fund run by BNP Paribas Asset Management that’s outperformed 97% of its peers over the past year says it’s now moving into small-modular-reactor stocks after long shunning the sector as a “non-viable” investment.

title: PGIM Bets on Hungary Extending Best Emerging-Market Bond Rally
summary: Hungary’s bonds, the world’s best emerging-market performers in the past months, are set to rally further, according to asset manager PGIM Ltd.

title: Political Backlash Over UK Police Response To Fatal Stabbing
summary: The police response to a fatal stabbing in the UK has sparked outrage across the political spectrum and prompted protests outside a police station. Henry Nowak was handcuffed as he lay dying in December after his killer, Vickrum Digwa, falsely accused him of racism. In bodycam footage, the 18-year-old can be heard telling officers, "I've been stabbed," to which one officer responds, "I don't think you have." Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he felt "sick" after watching the footage, while Reform UK Leader Nigel Farage said the reaction to the case should be one of "pure, cold rage." Bloomberg's UK Politics Reporter James Woolcock joined Caroline Hepker and Stephen Carroll to discuss the political fallout and growing public anger over the case. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Extends Gain as US-Iran Clashes Cloud Outlook for Peace Deal
summary: Oil rose for a third day as a fresh exchange of strikes between the US and Iran cast doubt on the prospects of a peace deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
